channel.title: Hypertrophy on Apoorva Sharma
channel.description: Recent content in Hypertrophy on Apoorva Sharma

item.title: Chest
item.description:
Anatomy

The pectoralis major has two heads: the clavicular (upper) and the sternal (lower/mid). Most pressing movements work both, but incline angle shifts emphasis toward the upper head.

Primary exercises

  • Flat barbell bench press — high load, bilateral, good for progressive overload
  • Incline dumbbell press — upper chest emphasis, greater stretch at the bottom
  • Cable fly (low-to-high) — constant tension, good finisher

Key cues

  • Retract and depress the scapula before unracking
  • Drive feet into the floor, maintain arch
  • Lower the bar to the lower chest, not the clavicle
  • Think “bend the bar” to engage lats and protect the shoulder

Programming notes

2–4 working sets, 6–15 rep range depending on goal. Chest responds well to volume; prioritise stretch-focused exercises for hypertrophy.

item.title: Back
item.description:
Anatomy

The back is a large, layered group: latissimus dorsi (width), traps (upper/mid/lower), rhomboids, and spinal erectors. Most people under-train the lower traps and erectors.

Primary exercises

  • Barbell row — high load horizontal pull, good for thickness
  • Pull-up / lat pulldown — vertical pull, lat width
  • Cable row (seated) — constant tension, good mind-muscle connection
  • Face pull — rear delts and upper traps, often neglected

Key cues

  • Initiate pulls by depressing the scapula, not by curling the wrist
  • On rows: lead with the elbow, not the hand
  • Full stretch at the top of pulldowns/pull-ups

Programming notes

Back tolerates high volume well. Include both vertical and horizontal pulls each week. Grip fatigue often limits performance — use straps when needed to train the target muscle rather than grip.
